---
title: "Curriculum Vitae"
---

You can download my full CV here: [Download CV (PDF)](files/CV_rownak.pdf){target="_blank"}

---

## Education

**The Ohio State University** | Columbus, OH

- Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering | Jan 2021 to Dec 2026 (expected)
  - Dissertation: *Aging-Aware Learning-Based Control for Electrified Powertrains*
  - Advisor: [Dr. Qadeer Ahmed](https://car.osu.edu/people/ahmed.358)
- M.S. in Mechanical Engineering | Dec 2023
  - Thesis: *Uncertainty in Multi-Objective Optimization: Weighted Entropy and Partition-Based Uncertainty Reduction*
  - Advisor: [Dr. Carol Smidts](https://mae.osu.edu/people/smidts.1)

**Bangladesh University of Engineering & Technology (BUET)** | Dhaka, Bangladesh

- B.S. in Mechanical Engineering | Oct 2018

---

## Professional Experience

### Powertrain and Electrification Controls (PTEC) Intern
*Cummins Inc.* | Summer 2026 | Columbus, IN

- Analyze operational data from heavy-duty commercial truck fleets to assess the hybridization opportunity for a P2 power-split architecture, characterizing adaptive cruise control usage patterns and quantifying recoverable braking energy from ACC-following scenarios.
- Build a MATLAB pipeline to decode and process multi-channel vehicle CAN bus logs, enabling fleet-scale identification of adaptive cruise control engagement modes across engine programs.
- Develop a physics-informed surrogate model of a heavy-duty truck adaptive cruise control decision layer, combining a car-following physics base with a data-driven residual correction to predict the controller's desired acceleration.
- Formulate a dynamic-programming framework to compute the fuel-optimal energy-management strategy for a Class 8 power-split hybrid powertrain, serving as the optimal benchmark for evaluating real-time hybrid controllers.

### Graduate Research Associate
*The Ohio State University, Center for Automotive Research* | Jan 2021 to Dec 2025

- Designed **physics-aware deep reinforcement learning** (SAC, PPO, GRPO) frameworks for hybrid-electric powertrain energy management, achieving fuel consumption within 1.1 to 4.8% of the dynamic programming optimum while reducing battery degradation by up to 88%.
- Developed a **Physics-Informed Transformer Model** for battery capacity fade prediction, reducing estimation error to 2.96% across 17 cells from 5 experimental datasets.
- Built and validated **physics-based aging models** for battery, electric machine, and aftertreatment system, integrated into a forward-looking MATLAB/Simulink powertrain simulator.
- Created a **Dynamic Programming** benchmarking framework to evaluate RL policy quality and generate expert trajectories for accelerated training.
- Proposed a **critic-free policy optimization** method (A-GRPO) using Transformer policies and trajectory-level advantage ranking for long-horizon constrained control.
- Demonstrated **embedded controller deployment** of trained RL policies on a Speedgoat real-time target in a controller hardware-in-the-loop (C-HIL) testbench (plant: dSPACE SCALEXIO, communication: CAN), reproducing training-time control behavior across 12 deployment runs with less than 1.34% fuel error.
- Delivered technical results to **Cummins Inc.** through regular milestone reviews and progress reporting.
- Designed a **multi-criteria sensor selection framework** for nuclear facility online monitoring systems.
- Developed a **VR-based experimental framework** for human reliability assessment under physical security threats.

### Graduate Teaching Assistant
*The Ohio State University* | Jan 2026 to Present

- Lead recitation sections and office hours for ME 3501: Introduction to Engineering Thermodynamics, supporting 100+ students in applying thermodynamic principles to engineering systems.

### Lecturer, Dept. of Mechanical Engineering
*Bangladesh Army University of Science and Technology (BAUST)* | Jun 2019 to Dec 2020

- Taught undergraduate courses: Numerical Analysis, Engineering Mechanics, Mechanics of Materials, and Measurement Instrumentation & Quality Control.
- Supervised student projects including design and performance analysis of a hydrodynamic tunnel and gravitational water vortex turbine.
- Served as Departmental Program Coordinator.

### Intern
*Navana Group* | Mar 2018 to Apr 2018 | Dhaka, Bangladesh

- Industrial internship in manufacturing processes and quality control.

---

## Selected Projects

**Aging-Aware Optimal Powertrain Control** | *Python, PyTorch, MATLAB/Simulink*

- End-to-end pipeline: physics-based aging model calibration → RL environment design → multi-objective reward engineering → policy training → DP-based evaluation, deployed on heavy-duty Class 8 series-hybrid drive cycles.

**Transformer-Based Battery Degradation Forecasting** | *Python, PyTorch*

- Trained a physics-informed Transformer on multi-cell cycling data (5 datasets, 17 cells) to predict capacity fade, outperforming LSTM and standard Transformer baselines.

**Critic-Free RL with Group-Relative Policy Optimization** | *Python, PyTorch*

- Developed A-GRPO for long-horizon constrained control tasks, eliminating the critic network while maintaining near-optimal performance; validated on powertrain control and OpenAI Gym benchmarks.

---

## Technical Skills

| Category | Skills |
|----------|--------|
| **Languages** | Python, MATLAB, C, C++ |
| **AI/ML** | PyTorch, TensorFlow, Scikit-learn; Transformers, RNN/LSTM; RL (SAC, PPO, TD3, GRPO, DDPG) |
| **Optimization** | Dynamic Programming, MINLP, MDP, NSGA-II, Multi-Objective Optimization, CasADi |
| **Modeling & Sim** | MATLAB/Simulink, LAMMPS |
| **Tools** | LaTeX, AutoCAD, SOLIDWORKS |

---

## Awards

- Graduate Fellow Award, Dept. of Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering, The Ohio State University (Autumn 2026)
- University Merit Scholarship, Dept. of Mechanical Engineering, BUET (2014)
- Dean's List Award, Dept. of Mechanical Engineering, BUET (2015, 2017)

---

## Professional Service

- Peer Reviewer, *IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification* (2026)
